text:
Market today: Mixed signals in tech. Memory crunch is the real driver today — Apple price hikes due to supply tightness ripple across semis and chip names, while Micron's blowout earnings (up 10%) then pullback shows how fast sentiment whips in this sector. Your kind of names are in the crossfire.

Your holdings: PLTR is your most urgent problem — down 17.66% and already flagged SELL; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, stop at $85 gives some room but don't let hope replace a plan. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.46% and flagged TRIM — memory crunch headlines don't help a chip name this size. SPAL is a 2x leveraged SpaceX ETF down 16% with daily decay eating you alive; it's a clock ticking. SPCX is a brand-new, highly volatile IPO with almost no price history — margin amplifies every gap.

Watch: How Micron's post-earnings fade holds (sets tone for AVGO/SMH). Whether Apple price-hike news spreads negative sentiment across chip demand.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is staying too long in leveraged and beaten-down names on margin — size down before the market does it for you.
